top_signals.title: Norway Averts Offshore Strike as Workers and Industry Reach Deal
top_signals.summary: Norway has narrowly avoided a strike at offshore oil platforms after trade unions and oil companies reached an agreement on wages in the early hours on Friday. The potential strike over wages could have threatened smooth operations offshore Norway, Western Europe's top oil and gas producer, at a time when the world is scrambling for oil and gas supply amid the Middle East crisis. Almost 8% of oil and gas workers offshore Norway early this week threatened to go on a strike from June 5 if trade union negotiations with industry fail to reach an agreement…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: India scraps tax on overseas bond investors in bid to attract foreign capital and shore up the rupee
top_signals.summary: India has announced several measures to boost capital inflows, including the scrapping of capital gains tax for foreign investors in government bonds.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Meet the big winners of 2026 oil price volatility — and why their strategies are now shifting focus
top_signals.summary: Computer-based trend-following hedge funds have successfully capitalized on huge price moves in energy markets.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Danantara Plans Low-Yield Bond as Investors Shun Indonesia
top_signals.summary: Indonesia’s sovereign wealth fund Danantara is planning to sell local-currency bonds that will pay interest below market rates at a time when global investors are rapidly losing confidence in the nation’s markets.
